title: Migrate relay wire protocol to JSON-RPC 2.0
description:
Reformat the nie relay WebSocket protocol from ad-hoc serde tag-discriminated messages to JSON-RPC 2.0. This is a breaking wire change that must land atomically — relay and client updated together.

## JSON-RPC 2.0 mapping

### Client → relay (requests, expect a response)
Each gets a client-generated integer `id`. Relay replies with matching `id`.

| Current type        | JSON-RPC method       | Response result         |
|---------------------|-----------------------|-------------------------|
| Authenticate        | "authenticate"        | {pub_id, subscription_expires} |
| Broadcast           | "broadcast"           | {message_id: uuid}      |
| GetKeyPackage       | "get_key_package"     | {pub_id, data: base64|null} |
| PublishKeyPackage   | "publish_key_package" | {ok: true}              |
| SetNickname         | "set_nickname"        | {ok: true}              |
| SubscribeRequest    | "subscribe_request"   | {chain, address, amount, expires} |
| Whisper             | "whisper"             | {ok: true}              |

Errors use standard JSON-RPC error object:
  {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"error":{"code":-32001,"message":"auth failed"}}

Custom error codes (>= -32000):
  -32001  auth_failed
  -32002  not_authenticated
  -32003  spoofed_sender
  -32004  nickname_taken

### Server → client (notifications, no id, no response expected)
| Current type        | JSON-RPC method       | params fields           |
|---------------------|-----------------------|-------------------------|
| Challenge           | "challenge"           | {nonce}                 |
| Deliver             | "deliver"             | {from, payload: base64} |
| DirectoryList       | "directory_list"      | {online, offline}       |
| UserJoined          | "user_joined"         | {pub_id, nickname, sequence} |
| UserLeft            | "user_left"           | {pub_id}                |
| UserNickname        | "user_nickname"       | {pub_id, nickname}      |
| KeyPackageReady     | "key_package_ready"   | {pub_id}                |
| WhisperDeliver      | "whisper_deliver"     | {from, payload: base64} |
| SubscriptionActive  | "subscription_active" | {expires}               |

### Special case: auth handshake
The Challenge notification fires immediately on connect (no request needed).
The client responds with an "authenticate" request. This is the one place
a notification triggers a request rather than the other way around.

## Wire examples
```json
// Server sends challenge (notification)
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"challenge","params":{"nonce":"abc123"}}

// Client authenticates (request)
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"authenticate","params":{"pub_key":"...","nonce":"abc123","signature":"..."}}

// Server confirms (response)
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"pub_id":"deadbeef...","subscription_expires":null}}

// Client broadcasts (request)
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"broadcast","params":{"payload":"aGVsbG8="}}

// Server acks (response)
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":{"message_id":"550e8400-..."}}

// Server delivers to other clients (notification)
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"deliver","params":{"from":"deadbeef...","payload":"aGVsbG8="}}

// Server error response
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"error":{"code":-32001,"message":"auth failed"}}
```

## Files that change
- core/src/protocol.rs  — replace RelayMessage enum with JsonRpcRequest / JsonRpcNotification / JsonRpcResponse / JsonRpcError structs
- core/src/transport.rs — update send/recv helpers to produce/parse JSON-RPC
- relay/src/ws.rs       — update handler to parse method+params, dispatch, return result/error with matching id
- cli/src/commands.rs   — update send calls and match arms to new types
- All tests that assert specific JSON shapes

## What does NOT change
- ClearMessage (inside payload) — unaffected, still tag-discriminated
- PaymentAction — unaffected
- Sapling/wallet code — unaffected
- The base64 encoding of payload fields — unchanged

## Acceptance
- cargo test --workspace passes
- Existing testnet E2E tests (TESTNET_ENABLED=1) pass against updated relay
- A client that sends the old format gets a -32700 parse error back
- Wire format matches JSON-RPC 2.0 spec exactly (validated by jsonrpc-validator or equivalent)
